questions.title: Can scrolling short videos before bed usually make it easier for your mind to enter a restful state?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.explanation: Constantly taking in stimulating content before bed may make your brain more alert and harder to calm down.

questions.title: When it is bedtime but you still want to use your phone, which choice is better?

options.text: Put your phone farther away
Keep scrolling in bed until you feel sleepy

questions.explanation: Putting your phone farther away can reduce the chance of picking it up unconsciously.

questions.title: Can using a very bright phone screen before bed affect falling asleep?

options.text: It might
Not at all

questions.explanation: A bright screen and stimulating content may make it harder to wind down before sleep.

questions.title: If you are already very sleepy, is continuing to scroll until you naturally fall asleep usually a good idea?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.explanation: The more you scroll, the easier it is to delay sleep and miss the moment when you were truly sleepy.

questions.title: Which activity is more suitable before bed?

options.text: Watching stimulating content
Doing something quiet and relaxing

questions.explanation: Lowering stimulation before bed can help your body enter a more restful state.

questions.title: Can fixed sleep and wake times help build better sleep habits?

options.text: Yes
Not really

questions.explanation: A stable routine helps your body build a consistent sleep rhythm.

questions.title: If you keep checking messages, comments, and short videos before bed, what may happen to your brain?

options.text: It becomes calmer
It becomes more alert

questions.explanation: Constantly refreshing content keeps the brain active.

questions.title: To reduce bedtime phone use, which method is more practical?

options.text: Rely only on willpower
Set a screen lock or limit in advance

questions.explanation: Setting rules in advance is usually easier than relying on willpower at bedtime.

questions.title: Does keeping your phone next to your pillow before bed usually increase the chance of using it?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.explanation: The easier your phone is to reach, the easier it is to open it without thinking.

questions.title: If you want to relax before bed, which choice is better?

options.text: Scroll short videos for an hour
Put down your phone and do a quiet activity for a few minutes

questions.explanation: Low-stimulation activities are more suitable as a bedtime transition.

questions.title: When you cannot stop scrolling before bed, what should you do first?

options.text: Scroll for another ten minutes
Put the phone down and pause

questions.explanation: Interrupting the action first is easier than bargaining with yourself to keep going.

questions.title: When using your phone before bed, does more stimulating content make it more likely to affect sleep?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.explanation: Stimulating content is more likely to pull your emotions and attention.

questions.title: What kind of sleep environment is more suitable?

options.text: Quiet, dim, and with fewer distractions
Bright, noisy, and full of notifications

questions.explanation: Quiet and fewer distractions help the body enter a resting state.

questions.title: What is the main purpose of automatic lock at bedtime?

options.text: Help you reduce continued phone use before bed
Make it easier for you to use your phone

questions.explanation: Automatic lock is designed to help you stop at the key moment.

questions.title: Can constantly switching between apps and content before bed make it harder for your brain to calm down?

options.text: Possibly
Impossible

questions.explanation: Frequent content switching increases stimulation and attention load.

questions.title: If it is already bedtime tonight, which choice is closer to a healthy habit?

options.text: Put the phone down first
Open another video

questions.explanation: Once it is bedtime, reducing new stimulation is better for falling asleep.

questions.title: Why is 'just a little while' often hard to keep when scrolling before bed?

options.text: Recommended content keeps bringing new stimulation
After one video, people usually fall asleep immediately
Scrolling in bed will not affect bedtime

questions.explanation: Recommendation feeds and messages can easily turn 'a little while' into a long time.

questions.title: What is most important when trying to build an early-sleep habit?

options.text: Decide day by day
Set a fixed plan in advance

questions.explanation: A fixed plan lowers the decision cost before sleep.

questions.title: If you feel bored or empty before bed, is phone scrolling always the best solution?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.explanation: Scrolling can briefly fill the emptiness, but it may also make you sleep later.

questions.title: Can reducing notification interruptions before bed help with sleep?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.explanation: Fewer notifications reduce the chance of being pulled back to your phone.

questions.title: If you want to protect your bedtime, which setting is more helpful?

options.text: Automatic bedtime lock
Opening more entertainment apps before bed

questions.explanation: Automatic lock can help cut off the path to continued scrolling at bedtime.

questions.title: As bedtime gets closer, is it more suitable to watch stimulating or tense content?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.explanation: Before bed, lowering stimulation is better than increasing alertness.

questions.title: What is one possible benefit of putting your phone outside the bed area before sleep?

options.text: It reduces picking it up casually
It makes it easier to scroll

questions.explanation: When the phone is not within reach, it is easier to avoid opening it unconsciously.

questions.title: If you unlock your phone after the lock period has already started, what does it mean?

options.text: Tonight's lock plan was interrupted
The lock plan was still fully completed

questions.explanation: Unlocking means this bedtime plan was interrupted.

questions.title: If you did not unlock last night, what does that suggest?

options.text: Your bedtime plan was followed well
It is completely meaningless

questions.explanation: Not unlocking means you protected this bedtime plan.

questions.title: When you want to use your phone before bed, what is the main purpose of stopping to answer a few questions first?

options.text: Interrupt the impulse and remind yourself to rest
Make yourself more excited

questions.explanation: The quiz is meant to pause the automatic scrolling state.

questions.title: Which sentence is more suitable as a bedtime reminder?

options.text: It is fine to scroll a bit more, it will stop automatically anyway
Put down the phone now and let your body rest

questions.explanation: Stopping in time before bed helps you rest better than continuing to scroll.

questions.title: Can drinking coffee or strong tea before bed affect falling asleep?

options.text: It might
Not at all

questions.explanation: Caffeine is stimulating, and intake in the afternoon or evening may affect sleep.

questions.title: Is eating a very full meal before bed usually helpful for falling asleep?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.explanation: A large meal before bed may keep your body busy digesting and affect comfort.

questions.title: Can sleeping in very late on weekends disrupt your usual rhythm?

options.text: It might
No

questions.explanation: Large changes in wake time can make your body rhythm less stable.

questions.title: If you wake up in the middle of the night and cannot sleep, which choice is better?

options.text: Immediately scroll on your phone
Keep stimulation low and wait for sleepiness to return

questions.explanation: Using your phone at night can make you alert again and make it harder to fall back asleep.

questions.title: Can constantly checking the time before bed make you more relaxed?

options.text: Yes
It may make you more anxious

questions.explanation: Repeatedly checking the time can increase pressure about not being asleep yet.

questions.title: Can appropriate daytime activity help nighttime sleep?

options.text: Yes
It has nothing to do with sleep

questions.explanation: Regular activity can help the body form a better rest rhythm.

questions.title: What kind of lighting is better when you want your body to get ready for rest?

options.text: Soft and dim
Very bright and harsh

questions.explanation: Lowering light stimulation before bed helps create a restful atmosphere.

questions.title: Can napping for too long affect falling asleep at night?

options.text: It might
Impossible

questions.explanation: Napping too long or too late may reduce sleepiness at night.

questions.title: If you are still dealing with work messages before bed, what may happen to your brain?

options.text: It may become more alert
It may immediately calm down

questions.explanation: Work messages can keep your brain thinking and make it harder to relax.

questions.title: When you feel anxious before bed, which approach is more suitable?

options.text: Keep scrolling to avoid it
Write down the worry and set it aside for now

questions.explanation: Writing worries down can sometimes help the brain temporarily let go.

questions.title: Alcohol may make people feel sleepy, but does it always improve sleep quality?

options.text: Yes
Not necessarily

questions.explanation: Alcohol may make you sleepy, but it can also affect sleep quality later in the night.

questions.title: To reduce bedtime procrastination, which approach is better?

options.text: Decide only at bedtime
Set a fixed bedtime routine in advance

questions.explanation: Planning the routine ahead reduces last-minute hesitation before sleep.

questions.title: Can a warm shower or foot soak before bed help some people relax and fall asleep?

options.text: It may help
It is definitely useless

questions.explanation: A gentle relaxing bedtime habit may help the body move toward rest.

questions.title: What is most important for a sleep plan?

options.text: Keep it as stable as possible every day
Decide entirely by mood
Rely only on weekend catch-up sleep

questions.explanation: Stable sleep and wake times are easier to turn into a habit.

questions.title: If you want to watch something before bed, which type is more suitable?

options.text: Continuous stimulating short videos
Low-stimulation content that is easy to stop
The more intense, the better

questions.explanation: The easier the content is to stop and the less stimulating it is, the less likely it is to delay sleep.

questions.title: If it is already very late tonight, which choice is more reasonable?

options.text: Start another new video
End this round of use first
Scroll until you fall asleep automatically

questions.explanation: Opening new content often pushes bedtime later.

questions.title: Can exposure to natural light after waking help adjust your routine?

options.text: It may help
It is meaningless

questions.explanation: Daylight helps the body distinguish day from night.

questions.title: If you receive many phone notifications before bed, which setting is better?

options.text: Turn on Do Not Disturb or reduce notifications
Let every notification ring
Put the phone by your pillow and wait for messages

questions.explanation: Reducing notifications lowers the chance of being pulled back to your phone.

questions.title: If you keep thinking about tomorrow's tasks before bed, what is a better approach?

options.text: Write them down briefly and handle them tomorrow
Lie there and think everything through

questions.explanation: Writing things down can help your brain set them aside for now.

questions.title: If you have been lying down for a long time and still cannot sleep, which choice is better?

options.text: Keep anxiously staring at the time
Keep stimulation low and help yourself relax

questions.explanation: Over-focusing on not sleeping may increase pressure.

questions.title: How can preparing tomorrow's things before bed help?

options.text: It reduces bedtime worry and repeated checking
It will definitely make you more excited

questions.explanation: Preparing ahead can reduce stray thoughts and unease before sleep.

questions.title: If you often scroll in bed for a long time, what association may your body build?

options.text: The bed is a place for being awake and entertained
The bed is only related to sleep

questions.explanation: If you often scroll in bed, the brain may find it harder to connect bed with sleep.

questions.title: If you suddenly want to watch a new video before bed, which choice better protects your sleep plan?

options.text: Do not open new content for now
Watch this one and decide later

questions.explanation: New content brings new stimulation and can push bedtime later.

questions.title: As bedtime approaches, what kind of reminder is more helpful?

options.text: Start bedtime preparation in advance
Force yourself to stop only when the time arrives

questions.explanation: A gradual transition is easier to carry out than a sudden stop.

questions.title: If you still want to move a little close to bedtime, what is more suitable?

options.text: Gentle stretching or relaxation
High-intensity exercise to exhaust yourself
Exercise while scrolling to stay alert

questions.explanation: Before bed, low-stimulation gentle movement is more suitable; intense exercise and phone use may make your body or brain more alert.

questions.title: If you feel lonely or empty before bed, which approach is gentler?

options.text: Fill the time with endless scrolling
Do a short, low-stimulation calming activity

questions.explanation: A low-stimulation transition can help you end the day more gently.

questions.title: If you often sleep too late or do not get enough sleep, what is more likely the next day?

options.text: Much more energy
More fatigue and lower attention
Coffee completely removes the effect

questions.explanation: Not getting enough sleep may affect your energy and attention the next day, and coffee cannot truly replace sleep.

questions.title: After staying up late, what may happen to your mood the next day?

options.text: More stable
More irritable or low

questions.explanation: Sleep loss can make emotional regulation harder.

questions.title: What is one benefit of entering bedtime preparation earlier?

options.text: It makes it easier to sleep according to plan
It makes endless procrastination more likely

questions.explanation: A transition period reduces the difficulty of suddenly stopping at bedtime.

questions.title: How may regular sleep help study and work?

options.text: It helps maintain attention and efficiency
One good night makes an irregular routine irrelevant
Regular sleep solves all efficiency problems

questions.explanation: Regular sleep usually supports focus and stable energy, but it is not the only factor in efficiency.

questions.title: What can frequent revenge bedtime procrastination lead to?

options.text: A brief sense of freedom tonight but more tiredness tomorrow
If it feels good tonight, tomorrow is usually unaffected
Weekend catch-up sleep completely solves it

questions.explanation: Revenge bedtime procrastination may briefly satisfy personal time needs, but it can affect the next day.

questions.title: When sleep-deprived, what may happen to decision-making and impulse control?

options.text: Impulses may be harder to control
You will definitely become more disciplined
It only affects the body, not emotions or choices

questions.explanation: Lack of rest can affect mood and self-control, making immediate rewards more tempting.

questions.title: If you want to improve sleep long-term, which is more important?

options.text: Going to bed very early once in a while
Keeping a stable routine as much as possible

questions.explanation: Sleep habits depend more on long-term consistency than on one occasional early night.

questions.title: Is drinking a lot of coffee the next day after staying up late the best solution?

options.text: Yes
No

questions.explanation: Caffeine may temporarily boost alertness, but it cannot replace actual sleep.

questions.title: If you sleep very late but still need to get up early, what is the most direct issue?

options.text: Your sleep time is compressed
Your sleep time becomes longer

questions.explanation: Late sleep and early wake-up reduce total sleep time and can make you more tired.

questions.title: What may regular sleep mean for physical recovery?

options.text: It helps recovery and maintaining body state
If you eat well, sleep does not matter
Occasional short sleep is fine, so long-term sleep does not matter

questions.explanation: Sleep is an important part of recovery; diet and rest matter and cannot fully replace each other.

questions.title: If your sleep time varies greatly every day, what may happen to your body rhythm?

options.text: It becomes more stable
It becomes more disrupted
As long as total time is similar, bedtime does not matter

questions.explanation: Large changes in sleep time may make it harder to build stable sleepiness and wake-up rhythms.

questions.title: In the long run, what does sticking to a bedtime lock plan mainly train?

options.text: A fixed bedtime habit
A habit of going to bed later
It is only a temporary phone limit and has nothing to do with habits

questions.explanation: Repeating the same bedtime boundary every night makes it easier to build a stable bedtime habit.

questions.title: How can nighttime screen light affect falling asleep?

options.text: It may suppress melatonin and affect circadian rhythm
Only because the phone gets warm
Only because of screen size

questions.explanation: Nighttime light, especially blue light, may affect melatonin and the body's signal that it is time to sleep.

questions.title: What is melatonin mainly related to in sleep?

options.text: Directly increasing muscle strength
Making people more excited during the day
Helping the body recognize night and sleepiness signals

questions.explanation: Melatonin is related to circadian rhythm and sleepiness signals; strong light at night can interfere with this process.

questions.title: Why can an irregular routine affect the body?

options.text: It makes body rhythms harder to stabilize
It is only psychological
It only affects dinner time

questions.explanation: Large swings in sleep and wake times can make it harder for the body to form a stable rhythm.

questions.title: Besides light, how else can phone use before bed affect sleep?

options.text: Stimulating content keeps the brain alert
The phone's weight changes your posture
More screen colors make you fall asleep faster

questions.explanation: Interactive content, short videos, and message feedback keep pulling attention and make the brain harder to calm.

questions.title: When sleep-deprived, why might emotions fluctuate more easily?

options.text: It only depends on the weather
Sleeping less definitely makes people happier
Lack of rest affects emotional regulation

questions.explanation: Sleep deprivation may make people more irritable, anxious, or emotionally unstable.

questions.title: What is a reasonable explanation for slower reaction the day after staying up late?

options.text: Sleep deprivation affects alertness and reaction speed
It means the body has fully adapted to staying up late
It is only because you skipped breakfast

questions.explanation: When you do not sleep enough, attention, judgment, and reaction speed may be affected.

questions.title: Why does a fixed wake-up time help sleep habits?

options.text: It helps stabilize the body's circadian rhythm
It is just for looking disciplined
It makes sleep unnecessary

questions.explanation: A fixed wake-up time gives the body a stable time signal and helps regulate the routine.

questions.title: What is a more accurate view of long-term sleep deprivation and health?

options.text: Young people do not need to care at all
It only makes people sleepy and does not affect the body

questions.explanation: Long-term sleep deprivation is linked with multiple health risks, though effects vary by person.

questions.title: Why does sleeping late and waking early make you more tired?

options.text: Total sleep time is compressed
Sleep quality definitely improves
The air is heavier in the morning

questions.explanation: If wake time does not change, going to bed late directly reduces available sleep time.

questions.title: What role does sleep play in memory and learning?

options.text: It only affects physical energy, not the brain
It clears all memories
It helps learning and memory consolidation

questions.explanation: Sleep supports learning and long-term memory formation; lack of sleep may affect thinking and focus.

questions.title: Why can sleep deprivation make you want to keep using your phone more?

options.text: Self-control and emotion regulation may weaken, making immediate rewards more appealing
Because the phone signal becomes stronger
Because the screen automatically looks better

questions.explanation: When under-rested, people may seek immediate feedback more strongly, making it harder to stop scrolling.

questions.title: Why can repeatedly checking messages before bed affect sleep?

options.text: More messages make the brain more relaxed
Reading messages does not use attention
The brain keeps processing social and task information

questions.explanation: Messages bring new tasks, emotions, or expectations, keeping the brain active.

questions.title: What is one benefit of following a sleep plan with a friend?

options.text: It is easier to get reminders and support
You will definitely never sleep late again
It has no effect at all

questions.explanation: Doing it with someone else can create external reminders and motivation to persist.

questions.title: Why can doing a new habit with someone else make it easier to keep?

options.text: Because your friend can sleep for you
Because you no longer need any effort
It increases companionship and commitment

questions.explanation: A friend cannot complete the habit for you, but can provide reminders, support, and some accountability.

questions.title: How should friend supervision for a sleep plan be understood?

options.text: Mutual punishment
Mutual control
Mutual support

questions.explanation: Good supervision is not control; it helps both sides do what they want to do more easily.

questions.title: When you want to unlock temporarily, what can knowing a friend will be notified do?

options.text: Help you pause and reconsider whether you really need to unlock
Make unlocking completely impossible
Let your friend decide for you

questions.explanation: The notification adds a pause and a sense of responsibility, not a total loss of choice.

questions.title: What problem is friend supervision best suited to help with?

options.text: You want to sleep early but waver at bedtime
All sleep problems
Problems that do not require sleep

questions.explanation: Friend supervision helps with execution, but it is not a substitute for professional sleep treatment.

questions.title: If your friend is also following a sleep plan, what feeling are you more likely to have?

options.text: Sleep does not need management at all
You must be perfect and never fail
You are not doing this alone

questions.explanation: Doing it together can provide companionship and reduce the feeling that you are the only one trying.

questions.title: Why is the bedtime habit suitable for a supervision friend?

options.text: Bedtime is when people often relax their guard and may need a little external reminder
Because people definitely have no self-control at bedtime

questions.explanation: Bedtime can be affected by fatigue and mood, so gentle external reminders may help.

questions.title: Why might telling a friend 'I will sleep earlier tonight' help?

options.text: Once you promise, you definitely will not fail
It makes the goal more concrete and easier to remember
The promise is more important than sleep itself

questions.explanation: Speaking the goal out loud can make you more aware that it is tonight's plan.

questions.title: If someone often procrastinates at bedtime, what can friend supervision provide?

options.text: External reminders and light accountability
A complete solution to all procrastination
Freedom from making any decisions

questions.explanation: External supervision can support action, but the user still needs to carry it out.

questions.title: What impact can seeing a friend is already locked have in the friend list?

options.text: It has nothing to do with your behavior
It makes you fall asleep immediately
It can motivate you to start locking too

questions.explanation: Seeing a friend follow the plan may create positive peer influence.

questions.title: If a user does not want to invite friends, can they still build a bedtime habit alone?

options.text: Yes, friend supervision is only support
No, without friends one cannot sleep early
Many people must supervise for it to work

questions.explanation: Solo use can also build habits; friend supervision just makes persistence more likely.

questions.title: Which statement is closest to the meaning of mutual supervision?

options.text: I manage your life
We keep the plan together
Your failures should be public

questions.explanation: Mutual supervision is about sticking with the plan together, not control or public judgment.

questions.title: When trying to build a new habit, how can telling a friend your goal help?

options.text: It makes the goal more concrete and easier to keep
The goal completes itself
You no longer need any effort

questions.explanation: Saying the goal out loud can create commitment, but you still need to act.

questions.title: If a friend can see your small daily progress, what may happen?

options.text: It is completely meaningless
It can create a little accountability
It will definitely make you overwhelmed

questions.explanation: Appropriately visible progress may help people take their goals more seriously.

questions.title: When building a habit, which approach is easier to keep?

options.text: Only think about it in your head
Rely entirely on your mood
Set a clear goal and involve others

questions.explanation: A clear goal plus external support makes steady action easier.

questions.title: Why can checking in together help?

options.text: Because others can complete it for you
Because a shared rhythm increases persistence
Because check-ins are more important than the goal

questions.explanation: The value of checking in together is building rhythm and companionship, not the form itself.

questions.title: When only you know a goal, what is more likely to happen?

options.text: It may be easier to quietly give up
It will definitely be completed better
The goal becomes simpler

questions.explanation: Without external reminders, people are more likely to give up or delay when they are not in a good state.

questions.title: Which approach is more suitable for building a long-term habit?

options.text: Do a lot once, then leave the rest to chance
Take small steps consistently and get appropriate feedback
Only act when you feel motivated

questions.explanation: Habits rely more on repetition and feedback than on one intense effort.
